_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The user encountered an authorization error while creating a change of address case. The system rejected their request because the 'ReceptionOfficer' role is not permitted to list these cases.

The user appears to be logged in as a ReceptionOfficer rather than a RegistrationOfficer. The authorization rules likely need updating to allow RegistrationOfficer access to change of address cases.</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

The error shows `ReceptionOfficer` is blocked from listing change-of-address cases while you're acting as a registration officer — I'll trace role assignment and the authorization rules for that endpoint.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The ReceptionOfficer role can create change of address cases but cannot list them. This permission gap likely causes the unauthorized access error.

The user may have been logged in as `ReceptionOfficer` rather than `PopulationOfficer`. Alternatively, a role switching bug could have caused the wrong role to persist. Another possibility is that the user reached the change-of-address page through an incorrect navigation path.

The reception officer may be redirected to the change-of-address list page after creating a case. That redirect would trigger the unauthorized access error.</details></think>

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The issue is located on the NewRegistrationCasePage.

The `/change-of-address` page restricts access to `PopulationOfficer` only. `ReceptionOfficer` roles are allowed to create change of address cases. This mismatch causes the unauthorized access error.

The user is a ReceptionOfficer, not a PopulationOfficer. The RegistrationRoleGate may redirect before OnInitializedAsync runs. The fix options are being evaluated.

Reception officers should not be redirected to the change-of-address list page. They likely need a direct dialog or a dedicated reception page instead. Allowing them to list cases would violate existing tests. The next step is to examine how the registration flow handles birth declarations for reception officers.</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The bug is located in `RegistrationRoleGate.razor`.

The `@if` check in `RegistrationRoleGate.razor` blocks child content for unauthorized roles. `ChangeOfAddressCaseList.razor` only allows `PopulationOfficer`. The `NavigateTo` redirect is asynchronous and does not block rendering. This causes the child component to render without the role gate allowing it.

The `@code` block belongs to the parent page component, not the child content inside the role gate. Because `ChangeOfAddressCaseList` is the root component, its `OnInitializedAsync` runs regardless of whether the child page renders. This explains why the API call executes even though the user lacks permission.

The page's `OnInitializedAsync` unconditionally calls `ReloadAsync()`, which triggers the API request before the role gate can block it. The unauthorized access error therefore occurs during initialization, while the asynchronous redirect from the gate is still pending.

The user described themselves as a "registration officer," yet the error shows a "ReceptionOfficer" role. This mismatch suggests the user may have been logged in under the wrong role, or the new-case creation flow may have restored incorrect session data.

The user likely confused the Population Officer role with the registration officer role. A true Population Officer would not trigger this error, because the new-case page redirects them to the cases list. The error instead indicates the user was logged in as a ReceptionOfficer, or the role detection is malfunctioning. Either way, the underlying bugs in the role gate and initialization logic still need fixing.

The new-case page redirects ReceptionOfficer to the change-of-address list, but that list is restricted to PopulationOfficer. A reception-friendly flow is needed for reception officers who create cases.

After a reception officer opens a case, they cannot view it either. The current permissions block them from both listing and viewing change of address cases.

The change of address dialog navigates to the case detail page after creation. That page also requires PopulationOfficer permission. The intended flow for a reception officer should instead show a local success card, similar to how birth declaration cases work on the new-case page. The OpenChangeOfAddressCase handler needs to follow the same direct-open-and-success pattern rather than redirecting to list or detail views.

The passport case page likely shares the same broken redirect pattern. The fix plan is to handle change of address inline on the new-case page for reception officers, and to guard ChangeOfAddressCaseList so it checks role before calling ReloadAsync.

Also considering a fix in `RegistrationRoleGate` itself, perhaps by moving API calls to `OnAfterRender` or preventing unauthorized pages from invoking APIs at all. The identity-documents page will be checked next to see how it handles `open=new` for reception officers.</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The reception flow routes users to `/change-of-address?open=new` from `NewRegistrationCasePage`. This triggers the unauthorized access exception.

The page crashes during initialization because `ReloadAsync()` requires `PopulationOfficer` role, but the user has `ReceptionOfficer`. `RegistrationRoleGate` redirects too late to prevent the exception. A `PopulationOfficer` would not hit this issue, since they can list change of address cases.

The user likely meant "reception officer" by "registration officer." Another possibility is that the URL retained a previous officer ID after a role switch. I will inspect `DemoOfficerPersistence` and `RoleSwitcher` to verify how the officer ID persists across navigation.</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_
